answer:
**This scene occurs in Cynewulf's *Juliana*, Chapter 1 (the only chapter).**

The heathen lord Eleusius (the persecuting reeve) orders Juliana's beheading after her ordeals by fire and boiling lead fail to kill her:[^1]

> Then the judge became fierce and grim-hearted,  
> and began to tear his robes. He gnashed his teeth  
> and ground them, went mad in his mind like a wild beast,  
> roared with a gallows-spirit and reviled his gods,  
> because they could not with their might withstand  
> a woman’s will. The glorious maid was  
> resolute and unafraid, mindful of her strength,  
> of the Lord’s will. Then the judge, sorrowful,  
> ordered the holy-hearted one slain by the sword’s bite,  
> her head taken from her, she who was chosen by Christ.  
> But that death did not profit him.[^1]

Terrified, Eleusius then flees by ship with his retinue across "the swan’s road" (a kenning for the sea), where all drown mid-voyage—including exactly **thirty-four** of his warriors—with the poet lamenting their lost hopes of hall-rewards from their "treasure-giver":[^2]

> Then her soul was led from her body  
> to the long-lasting joy, through the stroke of a sword.  
> Then that sinner, the terrified Eleusius,  
> with his troop of fiends, sought the sea-stream by ship,  
> and tossed over the flood for a long while  
> on the swan’s road. Death took them all,  
> that host of men, and himself with them,  
> before they had reached the land,  
> through a terrible torment. There thirty-four  
> of that warrior-kin were deprived of their lives  
> by the wave’s surge, lowly with their lord,  
> bereft of joy, without hope they sought hell.  
> Those thanes had no need to expect, in that dark home,  
> that company in the deep abyss, that their treasure-giver  
> would grant them rings in the wine-hall,  
> apple-gold from the beer-bench.[^2]

[^1]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [Chapter 1](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-7fd33e/).

[^2]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [Chapter 1](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-8ce9c2/).
